title: HypothesisRecord
description: Research Analyst Agent output — an implementation kit describing a technique extracted from a paper or repository. Designed for a developer to read and act on directly, not as a terse research lead. Every claim is grounded in verbatim_excerpts so the human can cross-check the extraction against the source. Confidence flags surface the agent's uncertainty field-by-field rather than hiding it.

title.description: Short technique name, suitable as a heading (e.g. 'Dynamic Vision Token Pruning via Attention Entropy').

source_citation.description: Bibliographic reference for the paper or repository this technique comes from.

claimed_effect.description: What the paper says the technique does, in 2-3 sentences. Written in the paper's own terms, not interpreted. Grounded in verbatim_excerpts.

verbatim_excerpts.description: Direct quotes from the paper describing the technique. At least one required. The human uses these to cross-check the agent's extraction against the source without re-reading the full paper.

original_hyperparameters.description: The paper's reported configuration as free-form text (e.g. a transcribed table, a list of values). Null when the paper does not report specific hyperparameters.

reported_results.description: The paper's claimed numbers, including the evaluation setup that produced them. Free-form text. Copied or paraphrased from the paper; not independently verified.

applicability_check.description: Structured assessment of whether this technique applies to our setup. Answers: does this work with our model size, data, hardware, and quantization?

known_failure_modes.description: Limitations and failure modes the paper itself discloses, typically from the discussion or limitations section. Empty list if the paper discloses none.

implementation_difficulty.description: Estimated effort to implement this technique in the project codebase. Determines Tier 1 (auto-runnable) vs Tier 2 (requires human implementation) routing. See HLD §6.3.

proposed_codebase_insertion_point.description: Which file or class in the codebase this technique would extend (e.g. 'runners/mlx_runner.py, VisionEncoder.forward'). A starting point for the human implementor, not final code. Null when the agent cannot determine a specific insertion point.

confidence_flags.description: Per-field uncertainty flags from the Research Analyst Agent. Keys are field names or concept names within this record; values are the agent's confidence level. An empty object means no uncertainty was flagged. Flags on claimed_effect, applicability_check, and original_hyperparameters are the most common.

SourceCitation.title: SourceCitation
SourceCitation.description: Bibliographic reference. At least one of url, arxiv_id, or github_url must be present to enable the citation verifier (HLD §6.4).

title.description: Full paper or repository title as it appears in the source.

authors.description: Author list in the order they appear in the paper.

venue.description: Conference, journal, or workshop name (e.g. 'ICLR 2025', 'NeurIPS 2024'). Null for preprints with no published venue.

arxiv_id.description: arXiv identifier (e.g. '2301.12345'). Enables direct arXiv API citation verification.

github_url.description: GitHub repository URL, if the technique has an official implementation.

url.description: Canonical URL for the paper (arXiv abstract page, ACL Anthology, conference proceedings). The citation verifier fetches this URL to confirm the paper exists. Required if arxiv_id and github_url are both null.

VerbatimExcerpt.title: VerbatimExcerpt
VerbatimExcerpt.description: A direct quote from the paper, with an optional location reference.

text.description: The verbatim quoted passage. Must be an exact quote, not a paraphrase.

location.description: Where in the paper this excerpt appears (e.g. 'Section 3.2, p. 5', 'Algorithm 1, line 4', 'Table 2'). Helps the human find the passage quickly.

ApplicabilityCheck.title: ApplicabilityCheck
ApplicabilityCheck.description: Structured assessment of whether this technique is applicable to the project's setup. The verdict is the agent's overall conclusion; requirements and notes provide the reasoning.

requirements.description: Explicit requirements the technique imposes (e.g. 'requires access to intermediate attention maps', 'requires model with ≥256 vision tokens', 'requires CUDA for custom CUDA kernels'). Extracted from the paper.

verdict.description: Overall applicability verdict. 'applicable': requirements are met by our setup. 'not_applicable': a hard requirement is not met. 'uncertain': applicability depends on factors not yet known.

notes.description: Reasoning behind the verdict. Explains which requirements are met, which are borderline, and what would change the verdict.
